metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `LLM-Review` label vote value vocabulary in the walkthrough (allowed integer values and the mapping of PASS, BLOCK/FAIL, and INDETERMINATE to those integers) co-located with the Step 4 label-configuration and Step 5 verdict→label-pipe sections.
findings.finding: The `LLM-Review` Gerrit label vote value vocabulary — the integer enum the bot posts — is never defined anywhere in the plan. The rebar kernel emits `PASS | BLOCK | INDETERMINATE` (confirmed in `src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json` and `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py`), but the mapping of these verdicts to Gerrit integer vote values (e.g., PASS→+1, BLOCK/FAIL→-1, INDETERMINATE→0) is absent. The ADRs that would define this (ADR-0013 for the label config, ADR-0009 for the verdict→label pipe) are confirmed absent from `docs/adr/` — the directory contains only ADRs 0001–0009 for unrelated topics.
findings.location: What section, Step 4: 'Configure the Gerrit project + LLM-Review label + submit requirement'; Step 5: 'the verdict->label pipe / code_review_decision seam'; AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a table in the guide (or in the referenced ADR, which must first be created) specifying: LLM-Review values −1 (BLOCK/FAIL), 0 (INDETERMINATE / abstain), +1 (PASS); and document that the submit requirement checks for a score ≥ +1, so INDETERMINATE does not satisfy the gate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a teardown/decommission section (or subsection of Step 8) to `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` covering `terraform destroy`, SSM parameter deletion, GitHub deploy-key revocation, and Gerrit project removal — completing the RETIRE leg of the shared-state lifecycle.
findings.finding: The walkthrough covers no RETIRE/teardown path for the shared AWS/SSM/Gerrit state it provisions. SSM SecureString parameters (`/rebar/prod/*`), EBS volumes, EIP, and IAM roles accumulate with no documented decommission procedure, leaving clients with orphaned billed resources and live secrets after they stop using the setup.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Decommission' subsection to Step 8 (Verify + Operate) that references `terraform destroy`, lists the SSM parameters to delete (`aws ssm delete-parameter --name /rebar/prod/*`), the GitHub deploy-key to revoke, and the GitHub branch-protection rule to restore to its previous state.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the INDETERMINATE verdict→Gerrit label handling rule (what vote value the bot posts, and what the submit requirement does with it) in the same section that defines the `LLM-Review` label and submit requirement.
findings.finding: The decision protocol for an `INDETERMINATE` rebar verdict at the `verdict→label` pipe (Step 5 / `code_review_decision` seam) is not co-located with the label schema and submit-requirement spec (Step 4). The rebar kernel explicitly degrades to INDETERMINATE on LLM unavailability (confirmed in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:414` and `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:331`), but what the bot posts to the `LLM-Review` label in that case — and whether the submit requirement then blocks all merges — is never stated in the plan.
findings.location: What section, Step 5: 'Wire the review-bot identity + webhooks + events-log — referencing ADR-0014 (inbound auth) + ADR-0009 (the verdict->label pipe / code_review_decision seam)'; Step 4: 'LLM-Review label + submit requirement'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a bot-down fallback specification to the walkthrough: define whether the `LLM-Review` submit requirement is configured as 'must have at least +1' (blocks on no vote) or 'any vote present ≥+1' (fails open on no vote), and document the recovery procedure when the bot cannot post a verdict.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for a review-bot failure to post any verdict (e.g., Anthropic API key expired, LLM service outage, bot container crash). Step 8 names a `/rerun` command and a kill-switch as post-deployment operations, but the plan never specifies the gating behaviour when the bot cannot complete a review — does Gerrit block all new patchsets from merging, or does the gate fail open?
findings.location: What section, Step 5: 'Wire the review-bot identity + webhooks + events-log'; Step 8: 'Verify + operate — the e2e check, the manual /rerun, the kill-switch, rollback'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or a requirement in the Step 6 walkthrough spec) that the guide explicitly state the plugin license basis — either by referencing ADR-0010's BSL-scope citation, or by noting that the Apache-2.0 status of the core `replication` push plugin was verified against the pinned 3.14.1 image license file (resolving the T1-critical open item recorded in d251 Round-11 remediation).
findings.finding: The plan instructs the walkthrough (Step 6 / AC1 step 6) to reference ADR-0010 for the Gerrit→GitHub replication plugin, but the parent epic (d251 Round-11 remediation, T1 critical) explicitly flagged that the Apache-2.0 claim for the core `replication` push plugin — vs. GerritForge's 2025-09-30 BSL re-license — must be CITED against published scope or verified by inspecting the LICENSE in the pinned 3.14.1 image. S8's plan does not require the walkthrough guide to carry forward, surface, or reference this license verification — leaving a client-facing document that propagates an unverified plugin-license assertion to a new operator.
findings.location: What — Step 6: 'Gerrit -> GitHub replication (deploy key, non-force) — referencing ADR-0010'; AC1 (step 6 coverage)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Step 6 description add: 'Note to implementer: the guide must include a license callout for the replication plugin referencing ADR-0010's BSL-scope verification (per d251 Round-11 T1-critical). If ADR-0010 does not yet contain the citation, add it before publishing the walkthrough.' Add a corresponding AC: 'The replication-step section of the guide cites the license basis for the core `replication` plugin (Apache-2.0, distinct from GerritForge's BSL re-license scope), per ADR-0010.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC or walkthrough step requirement that the guide documents the minimum-privilege IAM policy surface (specific actions/resources for the EC2 instance profile and SSM reader role) and prohibits wildcard `*:*` / AdministratorAccess shortcuts.
findings.finding: The plan's Step 2 mentions 'IAM' provisioning but never specifies that the walkthrough will document or verify minimum-privilege IAM scoping (no wildcard `*:*` or admin-for-convenience), leaving it entirely to ADR-0012 which does not exist in the repo.
findings.location: What — Step 2: Provision the AWS base; Acceptance Criteria — AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the Step 8 (Verify + operate) requirement to include: (a) a recommended CloudWatch alarm for disk utilisation and Gerrit process health, and (b) a teardown sub-section documenting `terraform destroy` sequencing and any manual pre-destroy steps (e.g., detach/snapshot EBS before destroy).
findings.finding: The plan specifies no observability requirements (CloudWatch logs/metrics/alarms) for the provisioned EC2/Gerrit infrastructure, and the 'Verify + operate' step omits a `terraform destroy` teardown path for the walkthrough.
findings.location: What — Step 8: Verify + operate; Acceptance Criteria — AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require the walkthrough to call out that the EBS volume resource in `infra/` must have a `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` block, and include a note in the verify/operate step about taking EBS snapshots before any `terraform apply` that touches EC2/storage.
findings.finding: The EBS volume (Gerrit's persistent data store) is a stateful resource, but the plan never mentions `prevent_destroy` lifecycle protection, nor does it address destroy/replace safety or dev/prod environment separation for the Terraform topology.
findings.location: What — Step 2: Provision the AWS base; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a walkthrough sub-step in Step 2 that specifies the Terraform remote-state backend contract (S3 bucket + DynamoDB lock table) and instructs operators to always run `terraform plan` and review the diff before `terraform apply`.
findings.finding: The plan never specifies that the walkthrough will document (or verify) that the Terraform `infra/` backend uses remote state with locking (e.g., S3 + DynamoDB), nor does it require `terraform plan` before `terraform apply` as a plan-before-apply discipline step.
findings.location: What — Step 2: Provision the AWS base (Terraform `infra/`)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Step 2 section of `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`, add: '**Before applying:** verify the `infra/backend.tf` S3+DynamoDB remote-state block is configured for your account; run `terraform plan -out=tfplan` and review the output — confirm no unexpected destroys — before running `terraform apply tfplan`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define or expand 'S6-pre' in the plan (e.g., name the specific script, ADR, or prior-sprint artifact it refers to) so the guide author can correctly reference it in step 7 of `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`.
findings.finding: Step 7 of the What section references 'ADR-0011 + S6-pre' but 'S6-pre' is never defined or explained anywhere in the plan, making it an opaque cross-reference that would carry into the guide and confuse readers trying to reproduce the setup.
findings.location: What section, step 7: 'Lock the GitHub repo to the replication identity — referencing ADR-0011 + S6-pre'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'S6-pre' with the actual artifact name, e.g., 'the GitHub branch-protection config produced in sprint S6' or 'ADR-0011 + the `scripts/lock-github-main.sh` script from infra/', whichever is accurate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cost & sizing callout to the Step 2 prerequisites section of `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` that documents the recommended instance type and EBS volume size, provides a rough monthly cost estimate, and notes that the EIP accrues charges when the EC2 instance is stopped.
findings.finding: The plan names EC2 + EBS + EIP as provisioned resources but never flags instance sizing, EBS volume sizing, or ongoing cost — leaving a client walkthrough silent on potentially unbounded or surprising AWS costs.
findings.location: What — Step 2: Provision the AWS base

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For each of the eight walkthrough steps in `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`, add a brief 'Troubleshooting / common failures' note (or a consolidated troubleshooting appendix) covering the most likely error state, its symptom, and the recovery action.
findings.finding: The walkthrough describes setup steps 2-7 only on the happy path; it does not enumerate failure/validation states for each step, leaving a client without guidance when a common setup error occurs (e.g., Terraform apply partial failure, TLS cert issuance failure, Gerrit webhook auth rejection, bot fails to post the LLM-Review label).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'When to use this guide / alternatives considered' section (or a Prerequisites callout) to the planned `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` that briefly states why the self-hosted Gerrit topology was chosen vs. simpler alternatives (e.g., GitHub Actions review-bot, managed Gerrit), so clients can self-select appropriately — satisfying T1(b) for the novel architecture the guide prescribes.
findings.finding: The client-facing walkthrough prescribes a non-trivial self-hosted topology (AWS EC2 + Gerrit + review-bot + nginx/TLS + Terraform + SSM) as the architecture for gating a GitHub repo with LLM code review. Simpler established alternatives exist (GitHub Actions + a review-bot workflow, GitHub App–based Gerrit-less LLM gate, cloud-hosted Gerrit Code Review service) but the plan does not require the guide to include a brief 'why this topology' rationale or 'when NOT to use this' section. A client-facing guide for a novel, operationally heavyweight architecture should justify the novelty vs. the simpler alternatives to satisfy T1(b) (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three).
findings.location: What — overall guide scope; AC1 (guide completeness); Why section
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add: 'The guide opens with a one-paragraph rationale ("when to use this topology") that states: (a) what the self-hosted Gerrit gate provides that GitHub Actions / a simpler bot cannot (deterministic submit-requirement, single vote, zero external service dependency on the vote path), and (b) the operational overhead trade-off (EC2 instance, Terraform, SSM, Gerrit admin), so a client can decide whether this fits their context before investing in the setup.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
